title: Mickaël Goudemand
wikitext:
{{short description|France international rugby league footballer}} {{Use dmy dates|date=October 2024}} {{Infobox rugby league biography |name = Mickael Goudemand |fullname = Mickaël Goudemand |image = Mickael Goudemand.jpg |image_size = 285 |position = Prop, 2nd row | birth_date = {{birth date and age|df=yes|1996|3|9}} | birth_place = [[Avignon]], [[Vaucluse]],
[[Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur]], France | height = {{convert|5|ft|10|in|m|abbr=on}} | weight = {{convert|15|st|6|lb|kg|abbr=on}} | club1 = [[SO Avignon]] | year1start = 2010 | year1end = 16 | appearances1 = 65 | tries1 = 12 | goals1 = 0 | fieldgoals1 = 1 | points1 = 49 | club2 = [[Dewsbury Rams]] | year2start = 2017 | year2end = | appearances2 = 8 | tries2 = 1 | goals2 = 0 | fieldgoals2 = 0 | points2 = 4 | club3 = {{nowrap|[[Catalans Dragons]]}} | year3start = 2018 | year3end = 23 | appearances3 = 99 | tries3 = 11 | goals3 = 0 | fieldgoals3 = 0 | points3 = 44 | club4 = [[Leeds Rhinos]] | year4start = 2024 | year4end = | appearances4 = 18 | tries4 = 1 | goals4 = 0 | fieldgoals4 = 0 | points4 = 4 | club5 = {{nowrap|[[Racing Club Albi XIII|Albi]]}} | year5start = 2025 | year5end = | appearances5 = 0 | tries5 = 0 | goals5 = 0 | fieldgoals5 = 0 | points5 = 0 | teamA = {{rlnt|France}} | yearAstart = 2018 | yearAend = present | appearancesA = 12 | triesA = 1 | goalsA = 0 | fieldgoalsA = 0 | pointsA = 4 |new = yes |source = [https://www.loverugbyleague.com/stats/players/mickael-goudemand/ loverugbyleague][http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/mickael-goudemand/summary.html Rugby League Project][http://www.itsrugby.co.uk/player_22777.html Its Rugby] | updated = 2 December 2024 }} '''Mickael Goudemand''' (born 9 March 1996) is a French professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} and {{rlp|lf}} for [[Junee Diesels]] in the [[Group 9 Rugby League]] and [[France national rugby league team|France]] at international level. Goudemand has previously played for [[SO Avignon]] in the [[Elite One Championship]] and the [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. ==Background== Goudemand was born in [[Vaucluse]], [[Avignon]], [[Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur]], [[France]]. ==Playing career== ===Catalans Dragons=== In 2018 he made his [[Challenge Cup]] début for the [[Catalans Dragons|Dragons]] against [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]].{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/44070522|title=Challenge Cup: Catalans Dragons 56-10 Whitehaven|work=BBC Sport }} In May 2018 he made his [[Super League]] début for [[Catalans Dragons|Catalans]] against the [[Leeds Rhinos]], scoring a try. He played in the [[2018 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/aug/25/catalans-dragons-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history|publisher=Guardian|date=25 August 2018|accessdate=5 November 2019}} On 9 October 2021, he played for Catalans in their [[2021 Super League Grand Final]] defeat against St. Helens.{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58858850|title=Super League Grand Final: Catalans Dragons 10-12 St Helens: Saints win three in a row|work=BBC Sport }} Goudemand played 17 games for Catalans in 2023 Super League season but did not feature in their [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] loss against Wigan.{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|newspaper=The Observer|date=14 October 2023 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }} ===Leeds Rhinos=== On 27 June 2023 it was reported that he had signed with [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] for the 2024 season on a two-year deal.{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=27 Jun 2023|accessdate=9 Oct 2023|title=Leeds Rhinos snap up France star from Catalans Dragons: 'He has his best years ahead of him'|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leeds-rhinos-mickael-goudemand-catalans-dragons}} He played 18 games for Leeds in the 2024 Super League season which saw the club finish 8th on the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/from-310-to-810-peter-smiths-leeds-rhinos-player-ratings-for-2024-season-4799226/|title=Leeds Rhinos player ratings from the 2024 Super League season|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk}} ===Racing Club Albi XIII=== On 28 Nov 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Racing Club Albi XIII|Albi]] in the [[Super XIII]]{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=28 Nov 2024|accessdate=2 Dec 2024|author=Callum Walker|title=Ex-Leeds Rhinos forward Mickael Goudemand finds new club|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-leeds-rhinos-forward-mickael-goudemand-finds-new-club/}} ===Junee Diesels=== On 21 June 2025 it was reported that he had signed for Australian [[Group 9 Rugby League]] side [[Junee Diesels]]{{cite web|website=Total RL|author=Stephen Ibbetson|date=21 Jun 2025|title=Former Catalans Dragons and Leeds Rhinos forward joins new club in Australia|url=https://www.totalrl.com/former-catalans-dragons-and-leeds-rhinos-forward-joins-new-club-in-australia/}} ==References== {{reflist|30em}} ==External links==
